mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and equity valuations over the following quarter. The positive growth trend is a persistent driver of higher stock prices.
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in at least 40% of quarters when GDPC1 shows positive growth, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, leading to negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return following UNRATE increases >0.5pp is not negative (one-sided t-test p >= 0.05), or fewer than 5 events occur in a 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a near-zero policy rate compresses real yields, driving investors out of fixed income into equities, which lifts the S&P 500 over the next month.
regime_hint: post-pandemic recovery with easy monetary policy
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return following trigger events is not positive in at least 70% of instances, or the average return is less than +1%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity and improving supply capacity, which supports equity valuations over the following quarter during the recovery phase.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery and early expansion phase
failure_condition: If the quarterly forward S&P 500 return following months with positive INDPRO growth is lower than the unconditional average during the hypothesized regime, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals a weakening housing market and consumer confidence, which often precedes broader economic slowdown and depresses equity prices over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: If the mean 5-day return following a >10% monthly drop in HOUST is not negative (one-sided test p >= 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving short-term S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: If the correlation between monthly changes in PAYEMS and forward 5-day returns is negative or insignificant (p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth alongside declining unemployment indicates robust consumer demand and income stability, which boosts corporate revenues and stock market returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return after trigger events is not positive in at least 60% of occurrences, or if average return is less than +0.5%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion phase, high and decelerating GDP growth signals approaching recession risk, leading market participants to reduce risk exposure and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with GDP growth above 3% and slowing
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day return when GDP growth is above 3% and decelerating (quarterly decline in growth rate) is not negative in more than 60% of cases, reject.
